steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The key signal: “However” introduces a completely different evolutionary stage—shifting from Pleistocene human ancestors to “the earliest multicellular organisms.” This directly indicates that Mistaken Point documents an older geological period.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D — It contains specimens from an older time period than those found in the formation in the Minatogawa quarry. directly expresses this time difference: Mistaken Point contains specimens from an older geological period than Minatogawa.

A, B, and C either invent unsupported details (fossil counts and species variety), misidentify organisms (human species), or overstate claims (“most valuable”).

That matches Choice D — It contains specimens from an older time period than those found in the formation in the Minatogawa quarry..
